title: Knockout of bla from pBlue in E. coli
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1hjri","text":"This protocol details the knockout of the bla gene from the pBluescript plasmid in Escherichia coli XL1-Blue by delivering a pre-assembled Cas9 ribonucleoprotein complex via electroporation. The method includes preparation of crRNA and tracrRNA, complex formation with Cas9, and subsequent transformation of electrocompetent cells.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3qot0","text":"Expected results are the generation of bacterial colonies with a disrupted bla gene and loss of ampicillin resistance, demonstrating efficient CRISPR/Cas9-mediated plasmid editing. This outcome confirms the possibilites of RNP-based CRISPR delivery as a strategy for targeted removal of antibiotic resistance markers in bacteria.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"45hmf","text":"Make sure to stick to the instructions very closely, especially if you are at the CRISPR part. There you'll need to work with extreme caution to prevent cross-contamination and eventually invalidate your results.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"1pgaf","text":"The components of this protocol are not designated as dangerous, however since you will be working with antibiotic resistant GMOs, the usage of safety gloves and a lab coat is highly recommended to prevent any contamination or similar.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ulre","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"96gbt","text":"Furthermore ensure that you plan your experiment according to the safety standards of your lab.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fvm1l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bciok","text":"Inform yourself about:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d7h64","text":"local waste disposal regulations","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2roe9","text":"lab specific operating instructions","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8mf6h","text":"lab specific safety procedures","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"51sn9","text":"more local restrictions when working with GMOs","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Retrieve XL-1 from the -80 °C freezer and let them thaw On ice
After thawing centrifugate 4500 rpm, 37 °C, 5 minutes
Seperate LB-media using pipettes
Resuspend XL1 pellets in 50 µg/µL electroporation buffer
Add 1 µL PBSII+
Incubate cells in a shaker at 200 rpm, 37 °C, 1 hour
Transfer 50 µL of the XL-1/buffer into a 0.1 cm electroporation cuvette You can also use a 0.2 cm cuvette for which you'll need to adapt the ep settings depending on your machine
Electroporate at 1.8-2.5 kV
Recover in 950 µL SOC at 37 °C for 01:00:00
Plate on selective LB-Amp plates using a sterile plating spatula Make sure to not re-use the spatula to avoid cross-contamination and dispose in the right waste
Incubate upside down at 37 °C Overnight Don't incubate longer than 18-24 hours When successful transformed, you should have multiple colonies growing on the plate
Pick one bacterial colony from the plate using the tip of the pipette

Resuspend in 450 µL LBM To further increase validity of the results, you can also pick three more colonies. Resuspend the first again in 450 µL LMB to serve as control colony. The other two are resuspended in 950 µL LBM, for which one again is used as control colony.
Incubate in a shaker 200 rpm, 37 °C, 10 minutes
Adjust crRNA concentration to 100 µMol for RNP preparation

Dilute lyophylized crRNA in buffer depending on n(crRNA) Aliquots can be stored @ -20 °C for 1 month or at -80 °C for longer periods Use the following formula to determine the required volume of buffer for 100 µMol c=\frac{n}{V} \to V=\frac{n}{c} Example: n(crRNA) = 2 nMol \frac{2\cdot10^{-9}\,\mathrm{mol}}{100\cdot10^{-6}\,\mathrm{mol\,L^{-1}}} =2\cdot10^{-5}\,\mathrm{L}=20\,\mu\mathrm{L}

Mix tracrRNA with crRNA in a ratio of 1.2:1.2 Work with extreme caution for the following section, since every μL can make a difference in the end result Make sure you're using separate tips for every step that follows to minimize the risk of unwanted dilution. Also ensure that you work according to lab specific genetic editing guidelines RNP complex consists of tracrRNA:crRNA:Cas9 in a ratio of 1.2:1.2:2.6 tracrRNA & crRNA were reconstitued in 100 micromolar (µM) each while the Cas9 is available in 40 micromolar (µM)
Incubate at 96 °C for 00:05:00
Cool down to Room temperature for around 00:10:00 Spin down at 13000 rpm, 37 °C, 3 minutes
In the meantime: bring Cas9 to Room temperature
Mix Cas9 to the sgRNA under continuous mixing
Incubate RNP complex at 500 rpm, 37 °C, 15 minutes, approx. 15 min Final RNP complex can be stored @ 4 °C for around 2 weeks and at -80 °C for longer periods
Mix 50 µL XL-1/pBSII+ 2-3 µL Cas9 RNP
Transfer to 0.1 cm cuvette It is also possible to use a 0.2 cm cuvette, however 0.1 cm cuvettes are preferred
Electroporate at 1.8-2.5 kV
Recover in 950 µL SOC at 400 rpm, 37 °C, 2 hours
Plate on selective LB-Amp plates It's recommended that you'll plate on a LB-agar plate as a control plate to validate your results. Furthermore it'll be helpful if you plate more than one plate

Incubate upside down at 37 °C Overnight Don't incubate longer than 18-24 hours If everything has been successful you should see a reduction of the colonies growing on the LB-Agar plate compared to the control plate. In the best case you shouldn't see any growing colonies on the LB-Amp plate
creator: Felix Hausmann
